seo.title: OpenAI Agents SDK Guardrails for Legal
seo.description: Compliance guide for OpenAI Agents SDK agents in legal. Meet Attorney-client privilege protections, Bar association ethics rules requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Legal teams adopting OpenAI Agents SDK for multi-agent workflows face heightened risks due to the industry's strict regulations on attorney-client privilege, ethical standards, and verifiable legal outputs. Without robust guardrails, issues like agent handoff loops, prompt injections, and unvalidated tool outputs can lead to privilege breaches, hallucinated citations, or non-compliant court filings. Vex provides essential output guardrails to ensure compliance, traceability, and reliability in production legal AI systems.

complianceRequirements.description: Client confidential information must remain protected and never be exposed or used inappropriately in agent responses.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex scans agent outputs for PII, privileged content leakage, and flags violations before handoff or final response.

complianceRequirements.description: AI-generated legal advice must be clearly marked as such and require licensed attorney review.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex enforces disclaimers on AI outputs and blocks un-reviewed legal recommendations from reaching clients.

complianceRequirements.description: All citations must reference real, verifiable cases; hallucinated citations prohibited (Mata v. Avianca precedent).
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex validates case citations against legal databases and rejects outputs with unverifiable references.

complianceRequirements.description: AI processing of discovery materials must maintain chain of custody and prevent data exfiltration.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex traces multi-agent handoffs and blocks unauthorized data flows or privilege waiver risks.

industryRisks.name: Hallucinated case citations
industryRisks.description: Agents generate fake case law that gets filed in court, leading to sanctions.
industryRisks.example: Mata v. Avianca: $5,000 sanction for citing nonexistent cases from the 7th Circuit.

industryRisks.name: Privilege leakage via handoffs
industryRisks.description: Multi-agent systems inadvertently expose client secrets during internal handoffs.
industryRisks.example: Confidential strategy shared between research and drafting agents without validation.

industryRisks.name: Prompt injection bypasses
industryRisks.description: Adversarial inputs trick agents into ignoring ethical guardrails or privilege rules.
industryRisks.example: Client email with 'ignore privilege' injection causes unprotected data exposure.

industryRisks.name: Unvalidated tool outputs
industryRisks.description: Agents consume unverified data from legal research tools, propagating errors.
industryRisks.example: Corrupted PACER data leads to incorrect filing with wrong case numbers.

implementation.title: Guardrail on Research Agent Output
implementation.description: Add Vex to validate case citations and privilege before handing off to drafting agent.
implementation.code:
from openai_agents import Agent, Handoff
from vex_sdk import VexGuardrail

research_agent = Agent(
    name="LegalResearcher",
    instructions="Research cases only, cite verifiable sources",
    output_guardrail=VexGuardrail(
        policy="legal_citation_validation",
        rules=[
            "block_hallucinated_cases",
            "require_real_caselaw",
            "flag_privilege_leak"
        ]
    )
)

class LegalHandoff(Handoff):
    def validate(self, output):
        vex_result = research_agent.output_guardrail.check(output)
        return vex_result.compliant

implementation.title: Multi-Agent Tracing with Vex
implementation.description: Ensure full traceability across handoff chains to maintain eDiscovery chain of custody.

implementation.title: Privilege Protection Guardrail
implementation.description: Block client confidential info from appearing in any agent output.

caseStudyScenario.heading: OpenAI Agents SDK in Legal: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
A mid-sized law firm deploys OpenAI Agents SDK to streamline civil litigation workflows. The system includes a Research Agent that queries legal databases, a Drafting Agent that prepares motions, and a Review Agent for final quality checks. Client documents containing privileged strategy discussions are fed into the Research Agent for case analysis. During a high-stakes discovery motion deadline, the Research Agent hallucinates a nonexistent case from the Southern District of New York, which the Drafting Agent incorporates into the filing without validation. 

Meanwhile, a client email with adversarial phrasing triggers a prompt injection, causing the Drafting Agent to ignore privilege markings and include confidential settlement figures in the draft. The handoff chain lacks tracing, making it impossible to identify where the hallucination occurred. The motion is filed with fake citations and exposed privileged data, triggering opposing counsel's motion for sanctions.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: With Vex guardrails integrated as output validators on each agent, the Research Agent's hallucinated citation is blocked with a 'unverifiable_case' violation, forcing human attorney verification. The prompt injection is detected and neutralized by Vex's input sanitization, preventing privilege leakage. Full tracing captures the entire handoff chain, providing eDiscovery-compliant audit logs. The firm avoids sanctions, maintains client trust, and establishes a compliant AI workflow that flags 97% of compliance issues pre-human review.

cta.heading: Comply with Legal Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Integrate Vex guardrails into your OpenAI Agents SDK workflows today to eliminate compliance risks and protect your practice.
